verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 (beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1) is a type II single-pass TM glycosyltransferase whose dominant localization is the trans-Golgi, where it galactosylates glycoproteins/glycolipids. However, it has a well-documented dual-localization minority pool on the cell surface (notably on sperm, where it acts as a zona pellucida receptor, and on somatic cells where it participates in cell-cell and cell-matrix adhesion). The surface form retains the same type II TM topology with the catalytic domain exposed extracellularly. This is a canonical example of Golgi/PM dual localization. Not `yes` because Golgi is the dominant compartment; surface pool is minor and context-dependent. Rules out: cancer/death ecto-pool not the main story; not germline-restricted (somatic surface pool exists); not lysosomal; tethering is via own TM anchor not partner.
